chapters.title: AgentVerse - 3D Multi-Agent Office Workplace
chapters.summary: Next.js + React Three Fiber **Tamil Nadu digital office** — agents sit at desks, walk over when summoned, and talk via the Agent Portal API

blocks.markdown:
Next.js + React Three Fiber **Tamil Nadu digital office** — agents sit at desks, walk over when summoned, and talk via the Agent Portal API at `:8080`.

**DEV train:** `feature/upgradation-functionality` · **0.3.0** (from `v0.2.2-stable`). Classic PREPROD/PROD stay on prior ship until Q1/Q2.

| Name | Role |
|------|------|
| Rajesh | Orchestrator (Office Lead) |
| Karthik | Research |
| Lavanya | Creative |
| Aravind | Coder |
| Meenakshi | Companion |
| Muthu | Project Manager |
| Kabilan | Device Lab QA (Chrome DevTools) |

Greetings default to **Tamil** (`ta-IN`); switch Hindi / English in the top bar.  
Prompts & routing: `src/prompts/personas.json`  
Crew / pre-work: `agents/`

chapters.title: Features
chapters.summary: Mandala office floor, holographic desks, work-loop animations

blocks.markdown:
- Mandala office floor, holographic desks, work-loop animations
- Click agent → stand → walk → greet → chat → return to desk
- Multi-project desk clusters (ask Rajesh: `new project: …`)
- Per-directory portal sessions (workspace picker + session tabs)
- **Session Desk** — search / filter / create / archive / restore / **cancel run**
- **Deep-links** — `/desk?intent=&crew=&brief=&return=` ([docs/DEEP-LINK-CONTRACT.md](docs/DEEP-LINK-CONTRACT.md))
- Quests bound to portal session status; incident brief strip on hire

- Node.js 22+ (host has 24.x)
- Agent Portal backend running on `http://127.0.0.1:8080`
- CSS on `:9000` when `cssEnabled` (DEV login: `admin` / `admin123`)
- **PREPROD/PROD login:** CSS admin password from `G:\apps\css\.env` (`CSS_ADMIN_PASSWORD`) — not `admin123` (staging returns 401)

```powershell
cd E:\MyWorkspace\agentverse-project
copy .env.example .env.local
npm run dev:stack
# Or UI only: npm run dev
```

Open `http://127.0.0.1:3310`.

PROD: https://agentverse.delena.buzz (`G:\apps\agentverse` :5310).

chapters.title: Deep-links
chapters.summary: See [DEEP-LINK-CONTRACT.md](./DEEP-LINK-CONTRACT.md). Prefer upgrade hosts for Dispatch return URLs when targeting this fleet.

blocks.markdown: See [DEEP-LINK-CONTRACT.md](./DEEP-LINK-CONTRACT.md). Prefer upgrade hosts for Dispatch return URLs when targeting this fleet.
[PROMOTE-UPGRADATION.md](./PROMOTE-UPGRADATION.md) · Evidence `H:\releases\agentverse-upgrade-0.3.0\`

**Status:** Frozen for Upgradation 0.2.4+  
**Hosts:** classic `agentverse.delena.buzz` / `agentverse-staging.delena.buzz` / DEV `:3310`  
**Keep compatible:** `/?session=<id>`

```
/desk?src=&crew=&session=&intent=&brief=&skills=&return=&env=&evidence=
```

| Param | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `src` | Caller id (e.g. `proddeck`) |
| `crew` | Persona id: `rajesh` … `kabilan` |
| `session` | Portal session UUID |
| `intent` | `session-desk` \| `hire` |
| `brief` | URL-encoded incident/hire text |
| `skills` | Comma skills (informational; future tabs) |
| `return` | Absolute URL back to Home/Watch — **allowlisted hosts only** |
| `env` | Hint `dev` / `preprod` / `prod` |
| `evidence` | Optional path/string for strip |

**Brief decode:** AV accepts URI brief (canonical) and legacy base64url during transition.
`return` must resolve to host in: `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, `home.delena.buzz`, `home-staging.delena.buzz`, `agentverse.delena.buzz`, `agentverse-staging.delena.buzz`. Others → ignored.

chapters.title: Behavior
chapters.summary: After CSS auth, AV parses query.

blocks.markdown:
1. After CSS auth, AV parses query.
2. `intent=session-desk` → open Session Desk.
3. `intent=hire` → select crew, open chat, seed compose from brief.
4. Brief → Incident strip (dismissible) + compose prefills `Investigate: …`.
5. `src=proddeck` → Incident strip banner **“ProdDeck dispatch params received”** even if `brief` empty (chat opens so strip is visible).
6. Safe `return` → “Back to Home” in chat/TopBar/IncidentStrip.

ProdDeck Dispatch must match this schema.

**When:** 2026-07-15  
**Branch:** `feature/upgradation-functionality` · **Version:** **0.3.1 LIVE**  
**Side ports:** **3312 / 4312 / 5312**  
**Hosts:** https://agentverse-upgrade-staging.delena.buzz · https://agentverse-upgrade.delena.buzz

packs.summary: What the product does — journeys, vision, upgradation behavior.

blocks.markdown:
**Project:** AgentVerse  
**Date:** 2026-07-12  
**Crew Lead:** Cursor CLI  
**Sources:** User requirements markdown; agent-portal migration; `E:\machine-docs\personas`

chapters.title: End-to-End Overview
chapters.summary: AgentVerse is the **Next.js + R3F/Three.js** successor shell that **migrates Agent Portal capabilities** (`https://github.com/sivaram311/age

blocks.markdown:
AgentVerse is the **Next.js + R3F/Three.js** successor shell that **migrates Agent Portal capabilities** (`https://github.com/sivaram311/agent-portal.git` — local SoT `E:\MyWorkspace\agent-portal`, mirror `agent-portal-ref`) into a **3D gaming hub**: lobby, quests/missions, animated Indian personas, PWA on Realme P2 Pro / Pad 2 / desktop.

**Orchestrator:** Rajveer routes tasks to Aarav (Research), Priya (Creative), Arjun (Coder), Meera (Companion) using `personas.json` prompts (aligned with `E:\machine-docs\personas` + app prompts).

Machine gates remain: CSS auth, port registry, schema-per-env, promote evidence.

chapters.title: User Journeys / Flows
chapters.summary: Enter the verse** — `npm run dev` / PWA → CSS login → 3D hub lobby.

blocks.markdown:
1. **Enter the verse** — `npm run dev` / PWA → CSS login → 3D hub lobby.
2. **Meet personas** — Rajveer / Aarav / Priya / Arjun / Meera (low-poly + LOD); tap/click → chat / mission assign.
3. **Quest / orchestrated task** — User goal → Rajveer routes to sub-agent(s) via prompts → progress as missions in HUD + avatar animation.
4. **Migrated portal flows** — Sessions, streaming, sub-agents, permissions, history/changes — gamified in 3D (API reuse vs reimplement TBD).
5. **Multi-device** — Touch orbit on phone/tablet; mouse on desktop; low-poly + LOD.
6. **Edge cases:** WebGL fail → 2D roster; auth fail; offline PWA shell.

chapters.title: Success Metrics
chapters.summary: `npm install && npm run dev` boots the 3D lobby.

blocks.markdown:
- `npm install && npm run dev` boots the 3D lobby.
- Five named personas interactive; Rajveer routes to others.
- Usable on Realme P2 Pro, Pad 2, desktop (PWA).
- Core agent-portal capabilities reachable or clearly phased with migration map.
- Production-ready: SemVer, health, CSS, reserved port, structured logs, Playwright smoke.

chapters.title: Non-Functional Requirements
chapters.summary: Stack:** Next.js + React Three Fiber / Three.js (locked).

blocks.markdown:
- **Stack:** Next.js + React Three Fiber / Three.js (locked).
- **Auth:** CSS (`:9000` DEV) — CONSCIOUS #8.
- **Ports:** Reserve before bind (`E:\MyAgent\workflow\ports\REGISTRY.md`).
- **DB:** Prefer reuse/adapt agent-portal persistence where practical.
- **Perf:** Low-poly models, LOD, touch/mouse controls.
- **Mobile QA:** Realme frames + `web-playwright-checklist.md`.
- **Drive:** DEV on E: only.

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Large agent-portal surface | Phased migration map in `02-architecture.md` |
| Empty `public/models/` | Procedural/low-poly placeholders first |
| Angular → Next rewrite | New UI; reuse Spring API where possible |
| 3D perf on phone | LOD, reduced draw calls, fallback 2D |

blocks.markdown:

2. Explicit **GO** to scaffold after you confirm #1 (or accept default: **proxy/reuse `:8080` first**).

chapters.title: Goal
chapters.summary: Make the stable office a trustworthy **operator work plane**: Session Desk findability, portal status/cancel truth, deep-link hire from Prod

blocks.markdown: Make the stable office a trustworthy **operator work plane**: Session Desk findability, portal status/cancel truth, deep-link hire from ProdDeck, honest quests, thin incident land — without restyling the 3D office.

chapters.title: Journeys
chapters.summary: Operator finds any session (search/filter) → opens → sees live status → can cancel.

blocks.markdown:
1. Operator finds any session (search/filter) → opens → sees live status → can cancel.
2. Home Dispatch opens `/desk?...` → AV lands with desk/hire/brief → return to Home.
3. Prompt creates a quest that tracks portal STREAMING/complete/fail — no fake %.
4. WAITING_PERMISSION / WAITING_PLAN surfaces real controls or honest Portal fallback.
5. Brief/incident strip on hire; dismiss; work continues in chat.

chapters.title: Project ↔ workspace binding
chapters.summary: SoT for this convention:** this doc + `src/lib/project-workspace.ts`

blocks.markdown:
**SoT for this convention:** this doc + `src/lib/project-workspace.ts`  
**Fleet:** agentverse-upgrade (`3312` / `4312` / `5312`) only

| Concern | Source of truth |
|---------|-----------------|
| Portal **sessions** (id, status, messages, permissions) | Agent Portal API |
| Office **projects** (desk clusters, crew, local UI) | AgentVerse zustand persist (`agentverse-office-v2`) |
| Project ↔ workspace path | `OfficeProject.workspacePath` (local durable field) |

Portal does **not** own office projects. AgentVerse does **not** invent a second session store — it only binds a project to a workspace path used when creating or switching sessions.

1. **Hub** (`id: "hub"`) → `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEFAULT_WORKSPACE` (default `demo`).
2. **Deployed project** → `{DEFAULT_WORKSPACE}/{slug(project.name)}`  
   - Slug: lowercase, non-alnum → `-`, max 48 chars (see `projectSlug`).
   - Separator follows the base (`/` vs `\`).
3. On `deployProject`, the resolved path is written to `OfficeProject.workspacePath` and becomes the active `workspacePath` in the store (also remembered in `recentWorkspaces`).
4. On `setActiveProject`, if the project has `workspacePath`, the global store `workspacePath` switches to it.

chapters.title: Session title convention
chapters.summary: When a portal session is created **for** an office project:

blocks.markdown:
When a portal session is created **for** an office project:

```text
AgentVerse · {project.name}
```

Helpers: `sessionTitleForProject`, `resolveProjectWorkspacePath` in `src/lib/project-workspace.ts`.

Chat / Session Desk may still use path-basename titles for ad-hoc workspaces that are not tied to a project.

chapters.title: UI
chapters.summary: `ProjectSwitcher` shows a short workspace hint (last path segment) and calls `setActiveProject`, which restores the bound path when present.

blocks.markdown: `ProjectSwitcher` shows a short workspace hint (last path segment) and calls `setActiveProject`, which restores the bound path when present.

chapters.title: Non-goals
chapters.summary: Creating directories on disk from the browser (portal / host enforces `AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT`).

blocks.markdown:
- Creating directories on disk from the browser (portal / host enforces `AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT`).
- Moving classic (`:3310`…) or stable-v2 (`:3311`…) fleets.
- Replacing portal session lookup by workspace path — `sessionsByPath` remains a local cache keyed by path.

| Changes / Files / Monaco | Later missions |
